title: Crepidomanes chevalieri
taxonomy: Crepidomanes chevalieri is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. The Hymenophyllaceae family is one of the nine families within the division Pteridophyta. The order of this family is Hymenophytales, which is a distinct order within the division Pteridophyta. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of Crepidomanes chevalieri with other ferns.
uses: Crepidomanes chevalieri, a species of fern, is known to have various medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. Additionally, Crepidomanes chevalieri has been used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and skin problems. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal uses and to ensure its safety for human consumption.

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
